title: Hemisphere Berry Flux of a Massive Dirac Hamiltonian Gives Half a Chern Number
summary: The lower band of H = p_x sigma_x + p_y sigma_y + m sigma_z defines a map from the compactified momentum plane to one hemisphere of S^2, so its Berry flux is one half of the sphere degree and equals sign(m)/2.
assumptions: The single-particle Hamiltonian is the continuum massive Dirac cone.
The occupied band is the lower-energy band.
regime: Continuum 2+1-dimensional Dirac cone viewed as a two-band Berry bundle.
scope: Geometric step behind the half-level induced Chern-Simons term in Lecture Three.

source_anchors.notes: Witten's hemisphere argument for the half-level term.
Geometric interpretation of the Berry curvature integral.

math_expressions.context: Normalized Bloch vector of the massive Dirac Hamiltonian.
Berry curvature of the occupied Dirac band.
Half-integer Chern number from the hemisphere map.

symbols.meaning: normalized Bloch vector of the two-band Dirac Hamiltonian

failure_modes: The half-integer result belongs to a continuum cone; a complete lattice model restores an integer total Chern number by supplying the missing half.
retrieval_hints: Use when a user asks where the factor of one half comes from geometrically.
